title: Python Backend Engineer
description: Python Backend Engineers design and maintain server-side application logic, REST APIs, data models, authentication flows, background jobs, and integrations that power web and mobile products.
responsibilities: Build secure REST API endpoints with validation, pagination, and useful error responses.
Design relational database schemas and write efficient SQL queries.
Implement authentication, authorization, caching, and background task patterns.
Write unit and integration tests for core business logic.
Debug production issues using logs, traces, database inspection, and reproducible test cases.
project_ideas: Build a job-application tracker API with authentication, PostgreSQL, filtering, and status history.
Create a FastAPI recommendation service that ranks roles by skills and stores search history.
Add Redis caching and background jobs to speed up repeated dashboard queries.
interview_questions: How would you design a REST API for search and filtering?
How do indexes improve database query performance?
How would you debug a slow endpoint in production?

title: Java Backend Engineer
description: Java Backend Engineers build scalable server-side services, enterprise APIs, database integrations, and business workflows using Java ecosystems such as Spring Boot.
responsibilities: Develop Spring Boot services for business workflows and API integrations.
Map domain models to relational databases using ORM and SQL.
Write tests for services, repositories, and API behavior.
Handle authentication, authorization, validation, and exception management.
Profile and optimize backend services for reliability and throughput.
project_ideas: Build a Spring Boot employee management API with role-based access control.
Create a banking transaction simulation service with validation and audit logs.
Design a microservice that exposes product search and inventory APIs.
interview_questions: What is dependency injection and why does Spring use it?
How do you handle transactions in a backend service?
How would you structure exception handling for an API?

title: Data Analyst
description: Data Analysts transform raw business data into reports, dashboards, insights, and recommendations that help teams make better operational and strategic decisions.
responsibilities: Clean, validate, and transform datasets for reporting and analysis.
Write SQL queries to join tables, aggregate metrics, and inspect anomalies.
Build dashboards that track KPIs and business trends.
Explain insights clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Document metric definitions, assumptions, and data quality limitations.
project_ideas: Analyze job postings to identify top skills by role and location.
Build a Power BI dashboard for sales, hiring, or student placement metrics.
Create a SQL case study with raw tables, cleaning steps, and final KPI report.
interview_questions: How would you investigate a sudden drop in weekly active users?
What is the difference between inner join and left join?
How do you make sure a dashboard metric is trustworthy?

title: Data Scientist
description: Data Scientists use statistics, machine learning, experimentation, and programming to model complex problems and generate predictions or decision-support insights.
responsibilities: Explore datasets and identify predictive signals or business patterns.
Train, validate, and compare machine learning models.
Evaluate models using appropriate metrics and error analysis.
Communicate model limitations, assumptions, and business impact.
Collaborate with engineers to deploy or operationalize model outputs.
project_ideas: Build a job salary prediction notebook with feature engineering and model comparison.
Create a churn prediction analysis with precision, recall, and business recommendations.
Analyze placement data and identify factors linked to successful outcomes.
interview_questions: How do you handle overfitting?
When would you use precision instead of recall?
How do you explain a model to a non-technical stakeholder?

title: React Frontend Developer
description: React Frontend Developers build interactive user interfaces, reusable components, client-side state flows, responsive layouts, and accessible product experiences.
responsibilities: Create reusable React components and predictable UI state flows.
Connect frontend screens to backend APIs with loading, error, and empty states.
Implement responsive layouts that work across mobile and desktop.
Improve accessibility using semantic HTML, keyboard support, and readable contrast.
Debug UI issues using browser dev tools and network inspection.
project_ideas: Build a career search dashboard with filters, result cards, and saved searches.
Create a component library for buttons, tabs, cards, modals, and form fields.
Design a responsive analytics dashboard connected to a mock API.
interview_questions: How does React state update and re-render a component?
How do you handle loading and error states for API data?
What makes a UI accessible?

title: Full Stack Developer
description: Full Stack Developers work across frontend, backend, databases, APIs, and deployment to deliver complete product features from user interface to persistent storage.
responsibilities: Design frontend screens and backend endpoints for complete features.
Model data in a database and expose it through reliable APIs.
Handle authentication, validation, and user-facing error states.
Deploy applications and manage environment configuration.
Balance user experience, code maintainability, and system reliability.
project_ideas: Build a full-stack placement portal with student profiles, job listings, and admin filters.
Create a project management tool with login, CRUD workflows, and dashboard charts.
Develop a knowledge-base search app with vector retrieval and user history.
interview_questions: How does data flow from the browser to the database and back?
How would you secure a full-stack app?
How do you decide whether logic belongs in the frontend or backend?

title: DevOps Engineer
description: DevOps Engineers automate build, test, deployment, monitoring, and infrastructure workflows so software teams can release reliable systems more frequently.
responsibilities: Create CI/CD pipelines that build, test, and deploy applications.
Containerize services and manage configuration through environment variables.
Monitor system health, logs, metrics, and incidents.
Automate infrastructure provisioning and release processes.
Improve deployment reliability, rollback safety, and operational documentation.
project_ideas: Containerize a FastAPI and React app with Docker Compose.
Create a GitHub Actions pipeline that runs tests and builds the frontend.
Deploy an API with health checks, environment variables, and monitoring notes.
interview_questions: What happens in a CI/CD pipeline?
How do containers differ from virtual machines?
How would you investigate a failed deployment?

title: QA Automation Tester
description: QA Automation Testers design automated test suites, validate product behavior, report defects, and improve release confidence through repeatable testing workflows.
responsibilities: Write test cases from product requirements and acceptance criteria.
Automate regression tests for critical user flows.
Test APIs, forms, edge cases, and error states.
Report bugs with clear reproduction steps and expected behavior.
Collaborate with developers to prevent repeated defects.
project_ideas: Create Playwright tests for login, search, filters, and dashboard states.
Build a Postman collection for a REST API and automate key checks.
Write a bug report portfolio with screenshots, steps, severity, and root cause notes.
interview_questions: What is the difference between smoke, regression, and integration testing?
How do you decide what to automate?
What makes a bug report useful?

title: Database Developer
description: Database Developers design schemas, write queries, optimize performance, enforce data integrity, and support applications that depend on reliable structured data.
responsibilities: Design tables, constraints, relationships, and indexes.
Write SQL queries for reporting, APIs, and data validation.
Optimize slow queries through indexing and execution plan analysis.
Create repeatable data migration and backup practices.
Protect data quality through constraints, validation, and documentation.
project_ideas: Design a normalized schema for a recruitment platform and write reporting queries.
Analyze a slow query and improve it with indexes and query refactoring.
Build an ETL script that cleans CSV data and loads it into PostgreSQL.
interview_questions: What is normalization and when might denormalization be useful?
How do indexes speed up reads and affect writes?
How would you protect data integrity in a schema?

title: Cloud Architect
description: Cloud Architects design secure, scalable, cost-aware cloud systems and guide teams on infrastructure, networking, deployment, reliability, and governance decisions.
responsibilities: Design cloud infrastructure for application workloads and data systems.
Select services based on reliability, cost, security, and team capability.
Define networking, identity, access, monitoring, and backup patterns.
Review system architecture and guide implementation tradeoffs.
Document diagrams, risks, operational practices, and scaling plans.
project_ideas: Draw and document a cloud architecture for a full-stack career dashboard.
Create an infrastructure plan with compute, database, storage, and monitoring choices.
Compare deployment options by cost, reliability, and operational complexity.
interview_questions: How would you design a scalable web application on cloud infrastructure?
How do you balance cost and reliability?
What security controls should a cloud application have?

title: UI/UX Developer
description: UI/UX Developers combine interface implementation, usability thinking, accessibility, and visual design detail to create product experiences users can understand and trust.
responsibilities: Translate designs into polished responsive interfaces.
Improve usability through clear information hierarchy and predictable controls.
Support accessibility with keyboard navigation, labels, and contrast.
Build reusable UI patterns and document component behavior.
Collaborate with designers, product owners, and backend developers.
project_ideas: Redesign a dashboard result card to show score, reason, source, and next action.
Build a responsive component gallery with forms, tabs, cards, and modals.
Run a Lighthouse accessibility audit and fix the highest-impact issues.
interview_questions: How do you turn a design into maintainable frontend code?
How do you test if an interface is accessible?
What makes a dashboard easy to scan?
